mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightness, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives immediate positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment decline, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in directional accuracy, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real estate activity combined with expanding consumer credit indicates a housing-led demand surge that historically precedes broad equity rallies, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain negative or flat in > 60% of observations where both housing starts and consumer credit rise, invalidating the demand-recovery premise.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in headline inflation above a monthly threshold triggers expectations of premature monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of instances following the inflation threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains wage-driven consumption and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of co-occurring events, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power beyond individual series in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent uptrend in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where industrial production trends upward, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, a contraction in consumer credit directly suppresses near-term retail demand, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of tightening episodes despite credit contraction, or the signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns in out-of-sample testing.
